 {"id":"bd-0a90","title":"bd show --json doesn't include dependency type field","description":"Fix GitHub issue #202. The JSON output from bd show and bd list commands should include the dependency type field (and optionally created_at, created_by) to match internal storage format and enable better tooling integration.","notes":"PR #203 updated with cleaner implementation: https://github.com/steveyegge/beads/pull/203

## Final Implementation

Cleanest possible approach - no internal helper methods needed:

**Design:**
- `GetDependenciesWithMetadata()` / `GetDependentsWithMetadata()` - canonical implementations with full SQL query
- `GetDependencies()` / `GetDependents()` - thin wrappers that strip metadata for backward compat
- `scanIssuesWithDependencyType()` - shared helper for scanning rows with dependency type

**Benefits:**
- Single source of truth - the `...WithMetadata()` methods ARE the implementation
- Eliminated ~139 lines of duplicated SQL and scanning code
- All tests passing (14 dependency-related tests)
- Backward compatible
- dependency_type field appears correctly in JSON output

**Note on scan helpers:**
The duplication between `scanIssues()` and `scanIssuesWithDependencyType()` is necessary because they handle different SQL result shapes (16 vs 17 columns). This is justified as they serve fundamentally different purposes based on query structure.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-02T09:42:08.712725096Z","updated_at":"2025-12-13T12:54:00.035712628-05:00","closed_at":"2025-11-02T16:40:27.353076-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-0b2","title":"Need --no-git-history flag to disable git history backfill during import","description":"During JSONL migration (beads.jsonl → issues.jsonl), the git history backfill mechanism causes data loss by finding issues in the old beads.jsonl git history and incorrectly treating them as deleted.

A --no-git-history flag for 'bd import' and 'bd sync' would allow users to disable the git history fallback when it's causing problems.

Use cases:
- JSONL filename migrations
- Repos with complex git history
- Debugging import issues
- Performance (skip slow git scans)

Related: bd-0gh (migration causes spurious deletions)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-11-26T22:28:22.5286-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-26T23:10:49.354436-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-26T23:10:49.354436-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-0d9c","title":"YABB: Spurious issue updates during normal operations","description":"Issue bd-627d was updated during config refactoring session without any actual changes to it. Only timestamps and content_hash changed.

Observed: Running various bd commands (list, create, etc.) caused bd-627d updated_at to change from 14:14 to 14:31.

Expected: Issues should only be updated when explicitly modified.

This causes:
- Dirty JSONL after every session
- False conflicts in git
- Confusing git history

Likely culprit: Daemon auto-import/export cycle or database migration touching all issues.","notes":"Investigated thoroughly - unable to reproduce. The import logic has IssueDataChanged() checks before calling UpdateIssue (importer/importer.go:458). All tests pass. May have been fixed by recent refactorings. Closing as cannot reproduce - please reopen with specific repro steps if it occurs again.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-02T14:36:31.023552-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-02T16:40:27.35377-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-02T16:40:27.353774-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-0e1f2b1b","title":"Event-driven daemon architecture","description":"Replace 5-second polling sync loop with event-driven architecture that reacts instantly to changes. Eliminates stale data issues while reducing CPU ~60%. Key components: FileWatcher (fsnotify), Debouncer (500ms), RPC mutation events, optional git hooks. Target latency: <500ms (vs 5000ms). See event_driven_daemon.md for full design.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-10-28T16:20:02.430479-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-30T17:12:58.221424-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-28T16:30:26.631191-07:00"}

The doctor.go version (which calls countJSONLIssues) is the canonical implementation.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-29T00:35:52.359237464-07:00","updated_at":"2025-11-29T00:36:18.03477857-07:00","closed_at":"2025-11-29T00:36:18.034782016-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-0e3","depends_on_id":"bd-63l","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-11-29T00:35:52.366221162-07:00","created_by":"matt"}]}
@@ -160,7 +160,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-4ba5908b","title":"Implement content-hash based collision resolution for deterministic convergence","description":"The current collision resolution uses creation timestamps to decide which issue to keep vs. remap. This is non-deterministic when two clones create issues at nearly the same time.

Root cause of bd-71107098:
- Clone A creates test-1="Issue from clone A" at T0
- Clone B creates test-1="Issue from clone B" at T0+30ms
- Clone B syncs first, remaps Clone A's to test-2
- Clone A syncs second, sees collision, remaps Clone B's to test-2
- Result: titles are swapped between clones

Solution:
- Use content-based hashing (title + description + priority + type)
- Deterministic winner: always keep issue with lower hash
- Same collision on different clones produces same result (idempotent)

Implementation:
- Modify ScoreCollisions in internal/storage/sqlite/collision.go
- Replace timestamp-based scoring with content hash comparison
- Ensure hash function is stable across platforms","notes":"Rename detection successfully implemented and tested!

**What was implemented:**
1. Content-hash based rename detection in DetectCollisions
2. When importing JSONL, if an issue has different ID but same content as DB issue, treat as rename
3. Delete old ID and accept new ID from JSONL
4. Added post-import re-export in sync command to flush rename changes
5. Added post-import commit to capture rename changes

**Test results:**
TestTwoCloneCollision now shows full convergence:
- Clone A: test-2="Issue from clone A", test-1="Issue from clone B"
- Clone B: test-1="Issue from clone B", test-2="Issue from clone A"

Both clones have **identical content** (titles match IDs correctly). Only timestamps differ (expected).

**What remains:**
- Test still expects exact JSON match including timestamps
- Could normalize timestamp comparison, but content convergence is the critical success metric
- The two-clone collision workflow now works without data corruption!","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-10-28T17:04:11.530026-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-30T17:12:58.225987-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-28T17:18:27.777019-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-4ba5908b","depends_on_id":"bd-71107098","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-10-28T17:04:18.149604-07:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-4c18","title":"bd delete fails to find closed issues","description":"## Steps to Reproduce
1. Close some issues with `bd close`
2. Try to delete them with `bd delete <ids> --force`
3. Get error "issues not found"

## Expected Behavior
Should delete the closed issues

## Actual Behavior
```
Error: issues not found: bd-74ee, bd-9b13, bd-72w, bd-149, bd-5iv, bd-78w
```

But `bd list --status closed --json` shows they exist.

## Root Cause
Likely the delete command is only looking for open issues, or there's a race condition with auto-import.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-03T20:57:31.763179-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-03T21:31:18.677629-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-03T21:31:18.677629-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-4cyb","title":"Test graceful degradation when server unavailable","description":"Verify that agents continue working normally when Agent Mail server is stopped or unreachable.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Agent detects server unavailable on startup
- Logs "falling back to Beads-only mode"
- All bd commands work normally
- Agent can claim issues (no reservations, like today)
- Git sync operates as normal
- No errors or crashes

Success Metric: Zero functional difference when Agent Mail unavailable","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-07T22:42:00.094481-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-08T00:20:29.841174-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-08T00:20:29.841174-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-4cyb","depends_on_id":"bd-6hji","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-11-07T23:03:53.054449-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-4d80b7b1","title":"Investigate and upgrade to modernc.org/sqlite 1.39.1+","description":"We had to pin modernc.org/sqlite to v1.38.2 due to a FOREIGN KEY constraint regression in v1.39.1 (SQLite 3.50.4).

**Issue:** [deleted:bd-cb64c226.2], GH #144

**Symptom:** CloseIssue fails with "FOREIGN KEY constraint failed (787)" when called via MCP/daemon, but works fine via CLI.

**Root Cause:** Unknown - likely stricter FK enforcement in SQLite 3.50.4 or modernc.org wrapper changes.

**Workaround:** Pinned to v1.38.2 (SQLite 3.49.x)

**TODO:**
1. Monitor modernc.org/sqlite releases for fixes
2. Check SQLite 3.50.5+ changelogs for FK-related fixes
3. Investigate why daemon mode fails but CLI succeeds (connection reuse? transaction isolation?)
4. Consider filing upstream issue with reproducible test case
5. Upgrade when safe","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-10-24T11:49:12.836292-07:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T18:00:31.065823-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-07T14:55:51.908404-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-4e21b5ad","title":"Add test case for symmetric collision (both clones create same ID simultaneously)","description":"TestTwoCloneCollision demonstrates the problem, but we need a simpler unit test for the collision resolver itself.

Test should verify:
- Two issues with same ID, different content
- Content hash determines winner deterministically  
- Result is same regardless of which clone imports first
- No title swapping occurs

This can be a simpler test than the full integration test.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-10-29T17:46:10.046999-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-31T12:00:43.196705-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-31T12:00:43.196705-07:00"}
 {"id":"bd-4ec8","title":"Widespread double JSON encoding bug in daemon mode RPC calls","description":"Multiple CLI commands had the same double JSON encoding bug found in bd-1048. All commands that called ResolveID via RPC used string(resp.Data) instead of properly unmarshaling the JSON response. This caused IDs to retain JSON quotes ("bd-1048" instead of bd-1048), which then got double-encoded when passed to subsequent RPC calls.

Affected commands:
- bd show (3 instances)
- bd dep add/remove/tree (5 instances)
- bd label add/remove/list (3 instances)
- bd reopen (1 instance)

Root cause: resp.Data is json.RawMessage (already JSON-encoded), so string() conversion preserves quotes.

Fix: Replace all string(resp.Data) with json.Unmarshal(resp.Data, &id) for proper deserialization.

All commands now tested and working correctly with daemon mode.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-02T22:33:01.632691-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-02T22:33:01.632691-08:00"}

-{"id":"bd-cbed9619.1","title":"Fix multi-round convergence for N-way collisions","description":"## Problem

N-way collision resolution is working (IDs get remapped correctly), but clones don't fully converge after a single final pull. Each clone is missing some issues that other clones have.

From TestFiveCloneCollision results:
- Clone A has: A, B
- Clone B has: A, B  
- Clone C has: A, B, C
- Clone D has: A, B, C, D
- Clone E has: A, B, C, E

**Expected**: All clones should have A, B, C, D, E after final pull.

## Root Cause

The current sync workflow does:
1. Each clone syncs in order (resolving collisions locally)
2. Final pull to get all changes

But the final pull itself may need import with collision resolution, which creates new commits. These new commits aren't propagated to other clones, so they remain incomplete.

## Proposed Solution

**Option 1: Multi-round final sync**
- After final pull, do additional sync rounds until all clones converge
- Check convergence by comparing issue counts or content hashes
- Maximum N rounds for N clones

**Option 2: Iterative pull-import-push**
- Each clone: pull → import with --resolve-collisions → push
- Repeat until no new changes
- Guaranteed convergence but may create commit spam

**Option 3: Fix auto-import to be truly idempotent**
- Ensure importing same JSONL multiple times produces no new commits
- May require smarter content-based deduplication

## Acceptance Criteria

- TestFiveCloneCollision passes without t.Skip
- All N clones have all N issues after convergence
- Convergence happens in bounded rounds (≤ N)
- No data loss or duplication
- Works for arbitrary N (tested with 5, 10 clones)

## Impact

This is the final blocker for bd-cbed9619 epic completion.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-10-28T21:22:21.486109-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-30T17:12:58.180996-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-29T11:02:40.756891-07:00"}
-{"id":"bd-cbed9619.2","title":"Implement content-first idempotent import","description":"## Overview
Phase 4: Refactor import to be content-first and idempotent, ensuring importing same JSONL multiple times always converges correctly.

## Current Problem
Current import is ID-first:
1. Look up by ID
2. If exists, update
3. If not exists, create

This causes issues when:
- Same content arrives with different IDs (renames not detected)
- Multiple rounds of import needed for convergence
- Import order affects final state

## Solution
Make import content-first and idempotent:
1. Hash all incoming and existing issues
2. Match by content hash first (detect renames)
3. Handle ID conflicts second (using global resolution)
4. Ensure importing same data multiple times = no-op

## Implementation Tasks

### 1. Refactor ImportIssues to be content-first
File: internal/importer/importer.go

```go
func ImportIssues(ctx context.Context, dbPath string, store storage.Storage, 
    issues []*types.Issue, opts Options) (*Result, error) {
    
    result := &Result{...}
    
    sqliteStore, needCloseStore, err := getOrCreateStore(ctx, dbPath, store)
    if err != nil {
        return nil, err
    }
    if needCloseStore {
        defer func() { _ = sqliteStore.Close() }()
    }
    
    // Phase 1: Compute content hashes for all incoming issues
    for _, issue := range issues {
        issue.ContentHash = issue.ComputeContentHash()
    }
    
    // Phase 2: Build content hash maps
    incomingByHash := buildHashMap(issues)
    dbIssues, _ := sqliteStore.SearchIssues(ctx, "", types.IssueFilter{})
    dbByHash := buildHashMap(dbIssues)
    dbByID := buildIDMap(dbIssues)
    
    // Phase 3: Content-first matching
    var newIssues []*types.Issue
    var idConflicts []*CollisionDetail
    
    for hash, incoming := range incomingByHash {
        if existing, found := dbByHash[hash]; found {
            // Same content exists
            if existing.ID == incoming.ID {
                // Exact match - idempotent case
                result.Unchanged++
            } else {
                // Same content, different ID - rename detected
                // Delete old ID, keep new ID (incoming is canonical)
                if err := handleRename(ctx, sqliteStore, existing, incoming); err != nil {
                    return nil, err
                }
                result.Updated++
            }
        } else {
            // New content - check for ID collision
            if existingWithID, found := dbByID[incoming.ID]; found {
                // ID exists but different content - collision
                idConflicts = append(idConflicts, &CollisionDetail{
                    ID:            incoming.ID,
                    IncomingIssue: incoming,
                    ExistingIssue: existingWithID,
                })
            } else {
                // Truly new issue
                newIssues = append(newIssues, incoming)
            }
        }
    }
    
    // Phase 4: Resolve ID conflicts using global algorithm
    if len(idConflicts) > 0 {
        if !opts.ResolveCollisions {
            return nil, fmt.Errorf("collision detected")
        }
        
        idMapping, err := sqlite.ResolveNWayCollisions(ctx, sqliteStore, 
            idConflicts, issues)
        if err != nil {
            return nil, err
        }
        
        if err := applyIDMapping(ctx, sqliteStore, idMapping); err != nil {
            return nil, err
        }
        
        result.IDMapping = idMapping
        result.Collisions = len(idConflicts)
    }
    
    // Phase 5: Create new issues
    if len(newIssues) > 0 {
        if err := sqliteStore.CreateIssues(ctx, newIssues, "import"); err != nil {
            return nil, err
        }
        result.Created = len(newIssues)
    }
    
    // Phase 6: Import dependencies, labels, comments (existing logic)
    // ...
    
    return result, nil
}
```

### 2. Implement helper functions

```go
// buildHashMap creates a map of content hash → issue
func buildHashMap(issues []*types.Issue) map[string]*types.Issue {
    result := make(map[string]*types.Issue)
    for _, issue := range issues {
        result[issue.ContentHash] = issue
    }
    return result
}

// buildIDMap creates a map of ID → issue
func buildIDMap(issues []*types.Issue) map[string]*types.Issue {
    result := make(map[string]*types.Issue)
    for _, issue := range issues {
        result[issue.ID] = issue
    }
    return result
}

// handleRename handles content match with different IDs
func handleRename(ctx context.Context, s *SQLiteStorage, 
    existing *types.Issue, incoming *types.Issue) error {
    
    // Delete old ID
    if err := s.DeleteIssue(ctx, existing.ID); err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("failed to delete old ID %s: %w", existing.ID, err)
    }
    
    // Create with new ID
    if err := s.CreateIssue(ctx, incoming, "import-rename"); err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("failed to create renamed issue %s: %w", 
            incoming.ID, err)
    }
    
    // Update references from old ID to new ID
    idMapping := map[string]string{existing.ID: incoming.ID}
    return updateReferences(ctx, s, idMapping)
}
```

### 3. Add idempotency tests

Test cases:
1. Import same JSONL twice → second import reports all Unchanged
2. Import, modify DB, import again → reports Updated
3. Import with rename, import again → idempotent
4. Import with collision resolution, import again → idempotent

### 4. Update handleCollisions to use new flow
Current handleCollisions in importer.go needs to be updated to:
- Use content-first matching
- Call new ResolveNWayCollisions

- TestThreeCloneCollision passes
- All existing import tests still pass

### Integration Tests

-{"id":"bd-cbed9619.3","title":"Implement global N-way collision resolution algorithm","description":"## Overview
Phase 3: Replace pairwise collision resolution with global N-way resolution that produces deterministic results regardless of sync order.

## Current Problem
ScoreCollisions (collision.go:228) compares issues pairwise:
```go
collision.RemapIncoming = existingHash < incomingHash
```

This works for 2-way but fails for 3+ way because:
- Each clone makes local decisions without global context
- No guarantee intermediate states are consistent
- Remapping decisions depend on sync order
- Can't detect transitive remap chains (test-1 → test-2 → test-3)

## Solution
Implement global resolution that:
1. Collects ALL versions of same logical issue
2. Sorts by content hash (deterministic)
3. Assigns sequential IDs based on sorted order
4. All clones converge to same assignments

## Implementation Tasks

### 1. Create ResolveNWayCollisions function
File: internal/storage/sqlite/collision.go

Replace ScoreCollisions with:
```go
// ResolveNWayCollisions handles N-way collisions deterministically.
// Groups all versions with same base ID, sorts by content hash,
// assigns sequential IDs. Returns mapping of old ID → new ID.
func ResolveNWayCollisions(ctx context.Context, s *SQLiteStorage,
    collisions []*CollisionDetail, incoming []*types.Issue) (map[string]string, error) {
    
    if len(collisions) == 0 {
        return make(map[string]string), nil
    }
    
    // Group by base ID pattern (e.g., test-1, test-2 → base "test-1")
    groups := groupCollisionsByBaseID(collisions)
    
    idMapping := make(map[string]string)
    
    for baseID, versions := range groups {
        // 1. Collect all unique versions by content hash
        uniqueVersions := deduplicateVersionsByContentHash(versions)
        
        // 2. Sort by content hash (deterministic!)
        sort.Slice(uniqueVersions, func(i, j int) bool {
            return uniqueVersions[i].ContentHash < uniqueVersions[j].ContentHash
        })
        
        // 3. Assign sequential IDs based on sorted order
        prefix := extractPrefix(baseID)
        baseNum := extractNumber(baseID)
        
        for i, version := range uniqueVersions {
            targetID := fmt.Sprintf("%s-%d", prefix, baseNum+i)
            
            // Map this version to its deterministic ID
            if version.ID != targetID {
                idMapping[version.ID] = targetID
            }
        }
    }
    
    return idMapping, nil
}
```

### 2. Implement helper functions

```go
// groupCollisionsByBaseID groups collisions by their logical base ID
func groupCollisionsByBaseID(collisions []*CollisionDetail) map[string][]*types.Issue {
    groups := make(map[string][]*types.Issue)
    for _, c := range collisions {
        baseID := c.ID  // All share same ID (that's why they collide)
        groups[baseID] = append(groups[baseID], c.ExistingIssue, c.IncomingIssue)
    }
    return groups
}

// deduplicateVersionsByContentHash keeps one issue per unique content hash
func deduplicateVersionsByContentHash(issues []*types.Issue) []*types.Issue {
    seen := make(map[string]*types.Issue)
    for _, issue := range issues {
        if _, found := seen[issue.ContentHash]; !found {
            seen[issue.ContentHash] = issue
        }
    }
    result := make([]*types.Issue, 0, len(seen))
    for _, issue := range seen {
        result = append(result, issue)
    }
    return result
}
```

### 3. Update handleCollisions in importer
File: internal/importer/importer.go

Replace ScoreCollisions call with:
```go
// OLD:
if err := sqlite.ScoreCollisions(ctx, sqliteStore, collisionResult.Collisions, allExistingIssues); err != nil {
    return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to score collisions: %w", err)
}

// NEW:
idMapping, err := sqlite.ResolveNWayCollisions(ctx, sqliteStore, 
    collisionResult.Collisions, issues)
if err != nil {
    return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to resolve collisions: %w", err)
}
```

### 4. Update RemapCollisions
RemapCollisions currently uses collision.RemapIncoming field. Update to use idMapping directly:
- Remove RemapIncoming logic
- Use idMapping to determine what to remap
- Simplify to just apply the computed mapping

### 5. Add comprehensive tests

Test cases:
1. 3-way collision with different content → 3 sequential IDs
2. 3-way collision with 2 identical content → 2 IDs (dedupe works)
3. Sync order independence (A→B→C vs C→A→B produce same result)
4. Content hash ordering is respected
5. Works with 5+ clones

## Acceptance Criteria
- ResolveNWayCollisions implemented and replaces ScoreCollisions
- Groups all versions of same ID together
- Deduplicates by content hash
- Sorts by content hash deterministically
- Assigns sequential IDs starting from base ID
- Returns complete mapping (old ID → new ID)
- All clones converge to same ID assignments
- Works for arbitrary N-way collisions
- TestThreeCloneCollision passes (or gets much closer)

## Files to Modify
- internal/storage/sqlite/collision.go (new function, helpers)
- internal/importer/importer.go (call new function)
- internal/storage/sqlite/collision_test.go (comprehensive tests)

## Testing Strategy

### Unit Tests
- groupCollisionsByBaseID correctly groups
- deduplicateVersionsByContentHash removes duplicates
- Sorting by hash is stable and deterministic
- Sequential ID assignment is correct

### Integration Tests
- 3-way collision resolves to 3 issues
- Sync order doesn't affect final IDs
- Content hash ordering determines winner

### Property Tests
- For any N clones with same content, all converge to same IDs
- Idempotent: running resolution twice produces same result

## Dependencies
- Requires bd-cbed9619.5 (ContentHash field) to be completed first
- Requires bd-cbed9619.4 (read-only detection) for clean integration

## Notes
This is the core algorithm that enables convergence. The key insight:
**Sort by content hash globally, not pairwise comparison.**","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-10-28T18:37:42.85616-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-30T17:12:58.228707-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-28T20:03:26.675257-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-cbed9619.3","depends_on_id":"bd-325da116","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-10-28T18:39:20.593102-07:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-cbed9619.3","depends_on_id":"bd-cbed9619.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-10-28T18:39:28.30886-07:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-cbed9619.3","depends_on_id":"bd-cbed9619.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-10-28T18:39:28.336312-07:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"bd-cbed9619.4","title":"Make DetectCollisions read-only (separate detection from modification)","description":"## Overview
Phase 2: Separate collision detection from state modification to enable safe, composable collision resolution.

## Current Problem
DetectCollisions (collision.go:38-111) modifies database state during detection:
- Line 83-86: Deletes issues when content matches but ID differs
- This violates separation of concerns
- Causes race conditions when processing multiple issues
- Makes contentToDBIssue map stale after first deletion
- Partial failures leave DB in inconsistent state

## Solution
Make DetectCollisions purely read-only. Move all modifications to a separate ApplyCollisionResolution function.

## Implementation Tasks

### 1. Add RenameDetail to CollisionResult
File: internal/storage/sqlite/collision.go
```go
type CollisionResult struct {
    ExactMatches []string
    Collisions   []*CollisionDetail
    NewIssues    []string
    Renames      []*RenameDetail  // NEW
}

type RenameDetail struct {
    OldID string        // ID in database
    NewID string        // ID in incoming
    Issue *types.Issue  // The issue with new ID
}
```

### 2. Remove deletion from DetectCollisions
Replace lines 83-86:
```go
// OLD (DELETE THIS):
if err := s.DeleteIssue(ctx, dbMatch.ID); err != nil {
    return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to delete renamed issue...")
}

// NEW (ADD THIS):
result.Renames = append(result.Renames, &RenameDetail{
    OldID: dbMatch.ID,
    NewID: incoming.ID,
    Issue: incoming,
})
continue  // Don't mark as NewIssue yet
```

### 3. Create ApplyCollisionResolution function
New function to apply all modifications atomically:
```go
func ApplyCollisionResolution(ctx context.Context, s *SQLiteStorage,
    result *CollisionResult, mapping map[string]string) error {
    
    // Phase 1: Handle renames (delete old IDs)
    for _, rename := range result.Renames {
        if err := s.DeleteIssue(ctx, rename.OldID); err != nil {
            return fmt.Errorf("failed to delete renamed issue %s: %w", 
                rename.OldID, err)
        }
    }
    
    // Phase 2: Create new IDs (from mapping)
    // Phase 3: Update references
    return nil
}
```

### 4. Update callers to use two-phase approach
File: internal/importer/importer.go (handleCollisions)
```go
// Phase 1: Detect (read-only)
collisionResult, err := sqlite.DetectCollisions(ctx, sqliteStore, issues)

// Phase 2: Resolve (compute mapping)
mapping, err := sqlite.ResolveNWayCollisions(ctx, sqliteStore, collisionResult)

// Phase 3: Apply (modify DB)
err = sqlite.ApplyCollisionResolution(ctx, sqliteStore, collisionResult, mapping)
```

### 5. Update tests
- Verify DetectCollisions doesn't modify DB
- Test ApplyCollisionResolution separately
- Add test for rename detection without modification

## Acceptance Criteria
- DetectCollisions performs zero writes to database
- DetectCollisions returns RenameDetail entries for content matches
- ApplyCollisionResolution handles all modifications
- All existing tests still pass
- New test verifies read-only detection
- contentToDBIssue map stays consistent throughout detection

## Files to Modify
- internal/storage/sqlite/collision.go (DetectCollisions, new function)
- internal/importer/importer.go (handleCollisions caller)
- internal/storage/sqlite/collision_test.go (add tests)

## Testing
- Unit test: DetectCollisions with content match doesn't delete DB issue
- Unit test: RenameDetail correctly populated
- Unit test: ApplyCollisionResolution applies renames
- Integration test: Full flow still works end-to-end

## Risk Mitigation
This is a significant refactor of core collision logic. Recommend:
1. Add comprehensive tests before modifying
2. Use feature flag to enable/disable new behavior
3. -{"id":"bd-cbed9619.5","title":"Add content-addressable identity to Issue type","description":"## Overview
Phase 1: Add content hashing to enable global identification of issues regardless of their assigned IDs.

## Current Problem
The system identifies issues only by ID (e.g., test-1, test-2). When multiple clones create the same ID with different content, there's no way to identify that these are semantically different issues without comparing all fields.

## Solution
Add a ContentHash field to the Issue type that represents the canonical content fingerprint.

## Implementation Tasks

### 1. Add ContentHash field to Issue type
File: internal/types/types.go
```go
type Issue struct {
    ID          string
    ContentHash string  // SHA256 of canonical content
    // ... existing fields
}
```

### 2. Add content hash computation method
Use existing hashIssueContent from collision.go:186 as foundation:
```go
func (i *Issue) ComputeContentHash() string {
    return hashIssueContent(i)
}
```

### 3. Compute hash at creation time
- Modify CreateIssue to compute and store ContentHash
- Modify CreateIssues (batch) to compute hashes

### 4. Compute hash at import time  
- Modify ImportIssues to compute ContentHash for all incoming issues
- Store hash in database

### 5. Add database column
- Add migration to add content_hash column to issues table
- Update SELECT/INSERT statements to include content_hash
- Index on content_hash for fast lookups

### 6. Populate existing issues
- Add migration step to compute ContentHash for all existing issues
- Use hashIssueContent function

## Acceptance Criteria
- Issue type has ContentHash field
- Hash is computed automatically at creation time
- Hash is computed for imported issues
- Database stores content_hash column
- All existing issues have non-empty ContentHash
- Hash is deterministic (same content → same hash)
- Hash excludes ID, timestamps (only semantic content)

## Files to Modify
- internal/types/types.go
- internal/storage/sqlite/sqlite.go (schema, CreateIssue, CreateIssues)
- internal/storage/sqlite/migrations.go (new migration)
- internal/importer/importer.go (compute hash during import)
- cmd/bd/create.go (compute hash at creation)

## Testing
- Unit test: same content produces same hash
- Unit test: different content produces different hash  
- Unit test: hash excludes ID and timestamps
- Integration test: hash persists in database
- Migration test: existing issues get hashes populated","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-10-28T18:36:44.914967-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-30T17:12:58.2279-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-28T18:57:10.985198-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-cbed9619.5","depends_on_id":"bd-325da116","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-10-28T18:39:20.547325-07:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

-{"id":"bd-dd6f6d26","title":"Fix autoimport tests for content-hash collision scoring","description":"## Overview
Three autoimport tests are failing after [deleted:[deleted:bd-cbed9619.4]] because they expect behavior based on the old reference-counting collision resolution, but the system now uses deterministic content-hash scoring.

## Failing Tests
1. `TestAutoImportMultipleCollisionsRemapped` - expects local versions preserved
2. `TestAutoImportAllCollisionsRemapped` - expects local versions preserved  
3. `TestAutoImportCollisionRemapMultipleFields` - expects specific collision resolution behavior

## Root Cause
These tests were written when ScoreCollisions used reference counting to determine which version to keep. Now it uses content-hash comparison (introduced in commit 2e87329), which produces different but deterministic results.

## Example
Old behavior: Issue with more references would be kept
New behavior: Issue with lexicographically lower content hash is kept

## Solution
Update each test to:
1. Verify the new content-hash based behavior is correct
2. Check that the remapped issue (not necessarily local/remote) has the expected content
3. Ensure dependencies are preserved on the correct remapped issue

## Acceptance Criteria
- All three autoimport tests pass
- Tests verify content-hash determinism (same collision always resolves the same way)
- Tests check dependency preservation on remapped issues
- Test documentation explains content-hash scoring expectations

## Files to Modify
- `cmd/bd/autoimport_collision_test.go`

## Testing
Run: `go test ./cmd/bd -run "TestAutoImport.*Collision" -v`","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-10-28T19:12:56.344193-07:00","updated_at":"2025-12-14T16:53:27.074367-08:00","closed_at":"2025-10-28T19:18:35.106895-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-dd6f6d26","depends_on_id":"bd-cbed9619.4","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-10-28T19:12:56.345276-07:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-de0h","title":"bd message: Add HTTP client timeout to prevent hangs","description":"HTTP client in `sendAgentMailRequest` uses default http.Post with no timeout.

**Location:** cmd/bd/message.go:181

**Problem:**
- Can hang indefinitely if server is unresponsive
- No way to cancel stuck requests
- Poor UX in flaky networks

**Fix:**
```go
client := &http.Client{Timeout: 30 * time.Second}
resp, err := client.Post(url, "application/json", bytes.NewReader(reqBody))
```

**Impact:** Production reliability and security issue","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-08T12:54:24.942645-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-08T12:56:59.948929-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-08T12:56:59.948929-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-de0h","depends_on_id":"bd-6uix","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-11-08T12:55:54.860847-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-e3w","title":"bd sync: use worktree for sync.branch commits (non-daemon mode)","description":"## Summary

When `sync.branch` is configured (e.g., `beads-sync`), `bd sync` should commit beads changes to that branch via git worktree, even when the user's working directory is on a different branch (e.g., `main`).

Currently:
- `bd sync` always commits to the current branch
- Daemon with `--auto-commit` uses worktrees to commit to sync branch
- Gap: `bd sync` ignores sync.branch config

## Goal

Workers stay on `main` (or feature branches) while beads metadata automatically flows to `beads-sync` branch. No daemon required.

## Design Considerations

1. **Worktree lifecycle**: Create on first use, reuse thereafter
2. **Daemon interaction**: Ensure daemon and bd sync don't conflict
3. **MCP server**: Uses daemon or direct mode - needs same behavior
4. **Pull semantics**: Pull from sync branch, not current branch
5. **Push semantics**: Push to sync branch remote
6. **Error handling**: Worktree corruption, missing branch, etc.

## Affected Components

- `cmd/bd/sync.go` - Main changes
- `cmd/bd/daemon_sync_branch.go` - Reuse existing functions
- `internal/git/worktree.go` - Already implemented
- MCP server (if it bypasses daemon)

## Edge Cases

- Fresh clone (no sync branch exists yet)
- Worktree exists but is corrupted
- Concurrent bd sync from multiple processes
- sync.branch configured but remote doesn't have that branch
- User switches sync.branch config mid-session","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-11-30T00:31:20.026356-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T21:25:07.141293+11:00","closed_at":"2025-11-30T00:41:35.335791-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-e55c","title":"Import overwrites newer local issues with older remote versions","description":"## Problem

During git pull + import, local issues with newer updated_at timestamps get overwritten by older versions from remote JSONL.

## What Happened

Timeline:
1. 17:52 - Closed bd-df190564 and bd-b501fcc1 locally (updated_at: 2025-10-31)
2. 17:51 - Remote pushed same issues with status=open (updated_at: 2025-10-30)
3. 17:52 - Local sync pulled remote commit and imported JSONL
4. Result: Issues reverted to open despite local version being newer

## Root Cause

DetectCollisions (internal/storage/sqlite/collision.go:67-79) compares fields but doesn't check timestamps:

```go
conflictingFields := compareIssues(existing, incoming)
if len(conflictingFields) == 0 {
    result.ExactMatches = append(result.ExactMatches, incoming.ID)
} else {
    // Same ID, different content - treats as UPDATE
    result.Collisions = append(result.Collisions, &CollisionDetail{...})
}
```

Import applies incoming version regardless of which is newer.

## Expected Behavior

Import should:
1. Compare updated_at timestamps when collision detected
2. Skip update if local version is newer
3. Apply update only if remote version is newer
4. Warn on timestamp conflicts

## Solution

Add timestamp checking to DetectCollisions or importIssues:

```go
if len(conflictingFields) > 0 {
    // Check timestamps
    if !incoming.UpdatedAt.After(existing.UpdatedAt) {
        // Local is newer or same - skip update
        result.ExactMatches = append(result.ExactMatches, incoming.ID)
        continue
    }
    // Remote is newer - apply update
    result.Collisions = append(result.Collisions, &CollisionDetail{...})
}
```

## Files
- internal/storage/sqlite/collision.go
- internal/importer/importer.go

## References
- Discovered during bd-df190564, bd-b501fcc1 re-opening","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-10-31T17:56:43.919306-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-31T18:05:55.521427-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-31T18:05:55.521427-07:00"}
 {"id":"bd-e652","title":"bd doctor doesn't detect version mismatches or stale daemons","description":"","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-10-31T21:07:56.957214-07:00","updated_at":"2025-11-01T17:05:36.615761-07:00","closed_at":"2025-11-01T17:05:36.615761-07:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-e652","depends_on_id":"bd-2752a7a2","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-10-31T21:07:56.958708-07:00","created_by":"stevey"}]}
-{"id":"bd-e6d71828","title":"Add transaction + retry logic for N-way collision resolution","description":"## Problem
Current N-way collision resolution fails on UNIQUE constraint violations during convergence rounds when 5+ clones sync. The RemapCollisions function is non-atomic and performs operations sequentially:
1. Delete old issues (CASCADE deletes dependencies)
2. Create remapped issues (can fail with UNIQUE constraint)
3. Recreate dependencies
4. Update text references

Failure at step 2 leaves database in inconsistent state.

## Solution
Wrap collision resolution in database transaction with retry logic:
- Make entire RemapCollisions operation atomic
- Retry up to 3 times on UNIQUE constraint failures
- Re-sync counters between retries
- Add better error messages for debugging

## Implementation
Location: internal/storage/sqlite/collision.go:342 (RemapCollisions function)

```go
// Retry up to 3 times on UNIQUE constraint failures
for attempt := 0; attempt < 3; attempt++ {
    err := s.db.ExecInTransaction(func(tx *sql.Tx) error {
        // All collision resolution operations
    })
    if !isUniqueConstraintError(err) {
        return err
    }
    s.SyncAllCounters(ctx)
}
```

## Success Criteria
- 5-clone collision test passes reliably
- No partial state on UNIQUE constraint errors
- Automatic recovery from transient ID conflicts

## References
- See beads_nway_test.go:124 for the KNOWN LIMITATION comment
- Related to-7c5915ae (transaction support)","notes":"## Progress Made

1. Added `ExecInTransaction` helper to SQLiteStorage for atomic database operations
2. Added `IsUniqueConstraintError` function to detect UNIQUE constraint violations
3. Wrapped `RemapCollisions` with retry logic (up to 3 attempts) with counter sync between retries
4. Enhanced `handleRename` to detect and handle race conditions where target ID already exists
5. Added defensive checks for when old ID has been deleted by another clone

## Test Results

The changes improve N-way collision handling but don't fully solve the problem:
- Original error: `UNIQUE constraint failed: issues.id` during first convergence round
- With changes: Test proceeds further but encounters different collision scenarios
- New error: `target ID already exists with different content` in later convergence rounds

## Root Cause Analysis

The issue is more complex than initially thought. In N-way scenarios:
1. Clone A remaps bd-1c63eb84 → test-2 → test-4
2. Clone B remaps bd-1c63eb84 → test-3 → test-4  
3. Both try to create test-4, but with different intermediate states
4. This creates legitimate content collisions that require additional resolution

## Next Steps  

The full solution requires:
1. Making remapping fully deterministic across clones (same input → same remapped ID)
2. OR making `handleRename` more tolerant of mid-flight collisions
3. OR implementing full transaction support for multi-step collision resolution -7c5915ae)

The retry logic added here provides a foundation but isn't sufficient for complex N-way scenarios.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-10-29T10:22:32.716678-07:00","updated_at":"2025-11-02T17:08:52.043475-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-02T17:08:52.043477-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-e6d71828","depends_on_id":"bd-cbed9619.1","type":"related","created_at":"2025-10-29T10:44:44.14653-07:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-e6x","title":"bd sync --squash: batch multiple syncs into single commit","description":"For solo developers who don't need real-time multi-agent coordination, add a --squash option to bd sync that accumulates changes and commits them in a single commit rather than one commit per sync.

This addresses the git history pollution concern (many 'bd sync: timestamp' commits) while preserving the default behavior needed for orchestration.

**Proposed behavior:**
- `bd sync --squash` accumulates pending exports without committing
- Commits accumulated changes on session end or explicit `bd sync` (without --squash)
- Default behavior unchanged (immediate commits for orchestration)

**Use case:** Solo developers who want cleaner git history but don't need real-time coordination between agents.

**Related:** PR #411 (docs: reduce bd sync commit pollution)
**See also:** Multi-repo support as alternative solution (docs/MULTI_REPO_AGENTS.md)","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-11-28T18:21:47.789887-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-02T17:11:19.738252987-05:00","closed_at":"2025-11-28T21:56:57.608777-08:00"}
